elements.sent-2411-001: In this paper, we prove two Liouville theorems for harmonic metrics on complex flat line bundles on gradient steady Ricci solitons and gradient shrinking K\"{a hler-Ricci solitons, which imply that they arise from fundamental group representations into $S^1$.}
elements.sent-2411-002: As is well-known, the classical Liouville theorem for harmonic functions states that there is no nontrivial positive harmonic function on $\mathbb{R ^n$.}
elements.sent-2411-003: It is Yau \cite{Ya1975 who successfully extended this theorem to the context of complete Riemannian manifolds with nonnegative Ricci curvatures nearly fifty years ago.}
elements.sent-2411-004: For $p>1$, Yau \cite{Ya1976 also proved that any nonnegative $L^p$ sub-harmonic function on a complete manifold must be a constant.}
elements.sent-2411-005: In particular, any harmonic function on a complete Riemannian manifold with nonnegative Ricci curvature must be constant if the energy is finite.
elements.sent-2411-006: Since then, the subject of Liouville properties for harmonic functions on complete Riemannian manifolds has witnessed a tremendous growth.
elements.sent-2411-007: This paper initiates the study of Liouville theorems for harmonic metrics on vector bundles.
elements.sent-2411-008: Let $(E,\nabla)$ be a vector bundle on a Riemannian manifold $(M,g)$, where $\nabla$ is a connection on $E$.
elements.sent-2411-009: Throughout this paper, unless indicated explicitly otherwise, vector bundles could be real or complex, whose metrics are Riemannian or Hermitian respectively.
elements.sent-2411-010:
Motivated by calculating the Euler characteristic number via the Gauss-Bonnet-Chern formula and its ramifications, one may ask: How to find metric compatible connections on $E$? We may write for a metric $H$ on $E$ such that  egin{equation} egin{split} 
abla=
abla_H+\psi_{H}, \end{split}\end{equation} where $
abla_H$ is a connection preserving $H$ and $\psi_{H}$ is an $\End(E)$-valued $1$-form.
elements.sent-2411-011: We define \begin{equation}\begin{split} \mathcal{E}_{\nabla}(H)=\frac{1}{2}\int_M|\psi_{H}|^2\dvol_g, \end{split}\end{equation} and the point is to minimize $\mathcal{E _{\nabla (H)$ when $H$ varies.}}
elements.sent-2411-012: A basic issue of harmonic metrics is to investigate its existence theory, there is a Riemannian Kobayashi-Hitchin correspondence for harmonic metrics analogous to the celebrated Kobayashi-Hitchin correspondence \cite{Do1985,Do1987a,UY1986 in complex geometry, which was well established by Corlette \cite{Co1988 and Donaldson \cite{Do1987b for flat vector bundles in the late 1980s, by Wu-Zhang \cite{WZ2023b in full generality for arbitrary vector bundles until very recently.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: On the other hand, recall the famous Chern conjecture predicts that any compact affine manifold has vanishing Euler characteristic.
elements.sent-2411-014: In particular, the Chern conjecture holds for a compact affine manifold whose tangent bundle is semi-simple and which admits a Riemannian metric with nonnegative Ricci curvature.
elements.sent-2411-015: A noncompact complete gradient Ricci soliton $(M,g,f)$ consists of a noncompact complete Riemannian manifold $(M,g)$, a smooth function $f$ (called the potential function), and a constant $\rho$ such that \begin{equation}\begin{split} \Ric_g+\nabla df=\rho g, \end{split}\end{equation} where we also denote by $\nabla$ the Levi-Civita connection on $TM$.
elements.sent-2411-016: After rescaling the metric we may assume that $\rho\in\{-\frac{1 {2 ,0,\frac{1 {2}\}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-017: Depending on the behavior of the Ricci flow on solitons, they are called shrinking if $\rho=\frac{1 {2}$, steady if $\rho=0$ and expanding if $\rho=-\frac{1 {2}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-018: Gradient Ricci solitons arise often as singularity models of the Ricci flow and that is why underlying them is an important question in the field.
elements.sent-2411-019: In this paper, we observe that Liouville theorems for harmonic metrics on complex flat line bundles on gradient Ricci solitons can be used to distinguish line bundles that arise from $S^1$-representations of fundamental groups.
elements.sent-2411-020: Let $(L,\nabla)$ be a complex flat line bundle on a noncompact complete gradient steady Ricci solition $(M,g,f)$ and $H$ be a harmonic metric with finite energy, then $\psi_H=0$.
elements.sent-2411-021: In particular, $\nabla$ is unitary and $L$ arises from a representation of $\pi_1(M)$ into $S^1$.
elements.sent-2411-022: Let $(L,\nabla)$ be a complex flat line bundle on a noncompact complete gradient shrinking K\"{a hler-Ricci solition $(M,g,f)$ and $H$ be a harmonic metric with finite energy, then $\psi_H=0$.}
elements.sent-2411-023: Similar results also hold for real flat line bundles, even though the above two theorems are stated for complex flat line bundles.
elements.sent-2411-024: Along the way of the proof, among other things, an important notion of pluri-harmonicit should be introduced as follows.
elements.sent-2411-025: Moreover, any harmonic metric is automatically pluri-harmonic due to Corlette, Deligne, Sampson, Simpson, Siu, see \cite{Co1988,Sa1986,Si1992,Siu1980 .}
elements.sent-2411-026: Crucially, we have the pluri-harmonicity of harmonic metrics on noncompact manifolds in the following sense.
elements.sent-2411-027: This rigidity phenomenon appears as a nonlinear system analogue of Li's classical result \cite{Li1990 for harmonic functions.}
elements.sent-2411-028: It may be mentioned that the pluri-harmonicity of harmonic metrics turns out to be a core property in studying the so-called nonabelian Hodge theory \cite{Co1988,Do1987b,Hi1987,Si1988,Si1992 on compact K\"{a hler manifolds and this property can be also investigated in various noncompact contexts by some other methods that presented in \cite{JZ1997,Si1988,WZ2023a .}}}
elements.sent-2411-029: If $\rho=0$ (that is $\Ric_g=\nabla df$), since every steady soliton is in particular an ancient solution to the Ricci flow, we have (see \cite{Ca2010,Ch2009 ) \begin{equation}\begin{split}\label{steady1} \Sca_g\geq0,\ \lambda\geq0,\ |\nabla f|\leq\sqrt{\lambda}. \end{split}\end{equation} On the other hand, Munteanu-Se\v{s um \cite{MS2013 proved the volume growth \begin{equation}\begin{split}\label{steady2} \frac{r}{c_1}\leq\vol_g(B_p(R))\leq c_2e^{\sqrt{R}},\ R>R_0, \end{split}\end{equation} for three uniform positive constants $c_1$, $c_2$, $R_0$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-030: Next let us consider the case $\rho=\frac{1 {2}$ (that is $\Ric_g=\frac{1 {2}\nabla df$), after normalizing we have \begin{equation}\begin{split}\label{shrinking1} \Sca_g+|\nabla f|^2=\lambda. \end{split}\end{equation} It is known from \cite{Ca2010,Ch2009 that $\Sca_g\geq0$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-031: On the other hand, Cao-Zhou \cite{CZ2010 proved that for any fixed $p\in M$, we have \begin{equation}\begin{split}\label{shrinking2} \frac{1}{4}(\dist(\bullet,p)-c_3)^2\leq f\leq\frac{1}{4}(\dist(\bullet,p)+c_3)^2, \end{split}\end{equation} for a uniform constant $c_3$.}
elements.sent-2411-033: We assume $(E,\nabla)$ is a vector bundle on a Riemannian manifold $(M,g)$ and $H$ is a metric on $E$.
elements.sent-2411-034: We set $A^{p (M,E)=\Gamma(\Lambda^pT^\ast M\otimes E)$, the space of $p$-forms on $M$ with values in $E$.}
elements.sent-2411-035: Associated to $\nabla$, the exterior differential operator \begin{equation}\begin{split} D: A^{p}(M,E)\rightarrow A^{p+1}(M,E) \end{split}\end{equation} is defined by requiring $D(\alpha\otimes u)=d\alpha\otimes u+(-1)^p\alpha\wedge\nabla u$ for any $\alpha\in A^p(M)$ and $u\in\Gamma(E)$.
elements.sent-2411-036: Then the curvature is given by $F_\nabla=D\circ\nabla\in A^2(M,\End(E))$.
elements.sent-2411-037: Using the Levi-Civita connection (also denoted by $\nabla$ for simplicity), the action of $\nabla$ may be further extended to tensorial combinations of $TM$ and $E$ as well as their duals.
elements.sent-2411-038: Then it holds \begin{equation}\begin{split} D\omega(e_0,...,e_p) =\sum\limits_{k=0}^p(-1)^{k}\nabla_{e_k}\omega(e_0,...,\hat{e_k},...,e_p), \end{split}\end{equation} where $e_1,...,e_p\in\Gamma(TM)$ and symbols covered by $\wedge$ are omitted.
elements.sent-2411-039: For a metric $H$ on $E$, we have the pointwise inner-product \begin{equation}\begin{split} <\cdot,\cdot>: A^p(M,E)\times A^p(M,E)\rightarrow C^\infty(M), \end{split}\end{equation} \begin{equation}\begin{split} (\omega,\theta)\mapsto\sum\limits_{i_1<...<i_p}H(\omega(e_{i_1},...,e_{i_p}),\theta(e_{i_1},...,e_{i_p})), \end{split}\end{equation} where $\{e_i\}_{i=1 ^{\dim M}$ is an orthogonal unit basis of $TM$.}
elements.sent-2411-040: Henceforth, we will omit the subscript $H$ if there is no ambiguity.
elements.sent-2411-041: The generalized inner-product \begin{equation}\begin{split} <\cdot,\cdot>: A^p(M,E)\times A^q(M,E)\rightarrow A^{p+q}(M) \end{split}\end{equation} is defined by requiring $<\alpha\otimes u,\beta\otimes v>=<u,v>\alpha\wedge\beta$ for any $\alpha\in A^p(M)$, $\beta\in A^q(M)$ and $u,v\in\Gamma(E)$.
elements.sent-2411-042: In particular, we have $<\omega,\ast\theta>=<\omega,\theta>\dvol_g$ for $\omega,\theta\in A^p(M,E)$, where $\ast$ is the Hodge star operator acting on the form component.
elements.sent-2411-043: If the base space is a K\"{a hler manifold, it is emphasized that the inner product on the complication tangent bundle we shall use is Hermitian.}
elements.sent-2411-044:
The connection $
abla$ is called unitary if  egin{equation} egin{split} d<u,v>=<
abla u,v>+<u,
abla v>, \end{split}\end{equation} where $u,v\in\Gamma(E)$.
elements.sent-2411-045: The co-differential operator \begin{equation}\begin{split} D_H^\ast: A^{p}(M,E)\rightarrow A^{p-1}(M,E) \end{split}\end{equation} is determined by $D_H$ (the exterior differential operator of $\nabla_H$) via \begin{equation}\begin{split} \int_M(D_H\omega,\theta)\dvol_g=\int_M(\omega,D_H^\ast\theta)\dvol_g, \end{split}\end{equation} where $\omega\in A^{p-1 (M,E),\theta\in A^p(M,E)$ and one of them is compactly supported.}

If $

elements.sent-2411-048: Let $p$ be a point of the base manifold $M$ and $\pi_1(M)$ be the fundamental group with base point $p$.
elements.sent-2411-049: Since $\nabla$ is flat, the parallel transport along a closed curve starting at $p$ depends only on its homotopy class and it gives a representation $\rho: \pi_1(M)\rightarrow GL(r)$ or $\rho: \pi_1(M)\rightarrow U(r)$ if $\nabla$ is unitary, where $r=\rank(E)$.
elements.sent-2411-050: Conversely, given a representation $\rho: \pi_1(M)\rightarrow GL(r)$, it corresponds to $E=\tilde{M}\times_\rho\mathbb{C ^r$ given by the universal covering space $\tilde{M}$ and the action of $[c]\in\pi_1(M)$ on $(q,v)\in\tilde{M}\times\mathbb{C ^r$ via $[c]\cdot(q,v)=([c]\cdot q,\rho([c])(v))$.}}
elements.sent-2411-051: Then we say the flat vector bundle $E$ arises from the fundamental group representation $\rho$.

elements.sent-2411-064: We prove that there is no nontrivial $L^2$-integrable harmonic 1-form on noncompact complete gradient steady Ricci solitons or noncompact complete gradient shrinking K\"{a hler-Ricci solitons.}
elements.sent-2411-065: As an application, it can be used to distinguish certain flat vector bundles that arise from fundamental group representations into $\SL(r,\mathbb{C )$.}
elements.sent-2411-066: Using the logarithmic gradient estimate, it is Yau \cite{Ya1975 who successfully extended this theorem to the context of noncompact complete manifolds with nonnegative Ricci curvatures nearly fifty years ago.}
elements.sent-2411-067: Yau \cite{Ya1976 also proved that any nonnegative $L^p$-integrable sub-harmonic function on a complete manifold must be a constant when $p>1$, while Li-Schoen \cite{LS1984 obtained similar result on noncompact complete manifolds with nonnegative Ricci curvatures when $p\in(0,1]$.}}
elements.sent-2411-068: In particular, there is no nontrivial harmonic function with finite energy on a complete manifold with nonnegative Ricci curvature.
elements.sent-2411-069: Since then the subject has witnessed a tremendous growth, the reader may consult \cite{CW2019,Li2006 for the comprehensive exposition of Liouville properties for harmonic functions as well as related topics.}
elements.sent-2411-070: Motivated by Liouville theorems for harmonic functions on gradient Ricci solitons studied by Munteanu-Se\v{s um \cite{MS2013 , we want to investigate Liouville theorems for harmonic 1-forms on gradient Ricci solitons.}}
elements.sent-2411-071: A noncompact complete gradient Ricci soliton $(M,g,f)$ consists of a noncompact complete Riemannian manifold $(M,g)$, a smooth function $f$ (called the potential function), and a constant $\rho$ such that \begin{equation}\begin{split} \Ric_g+\nabla df=\rho g, \end{split}\end{equation} where we denote by $\nabla$ the Levi-Civita connection.
elements.sent-2411-072: It is called shrinking if $\rho=\frac{1 {2}$, steady if $\rho=0$ and expanding if $\rho=-\frac{1 {2}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-073: Clearly, a gradient Ricci soliton is simply an Einstein manifold if $f$ is a constant function.
elements.sent-2411-074: Gradient Ricci solitons are not only the generalization of Einstein manifolds but also arise often as singularity models of the Ricci flow and that is why underlying them is an important question in the field.
elements.sent-2411-075: So it is quite natural to ask whether many known Liouville type properties on noncompact complete manifolds with nonnegative Ricci curvatures can be extended to the context of noncompact complete gradient steady Ricci solitons or noncompact complete gradient shrinking Ricci solitons.
elements.sent-2411-076: It should be mentioned that the issue for standard harmonic objects sometimes seems more delicate and many results remain unclearly.
elements.sent-2411-077: In this direction, Munteanu-Sesum \cite{MS2013 obtained the corresponding result for harmonic functions with finite energies.}
elements.sent-2411-078: The issue still deserves further research for harmonic functions with point-wise conditions, see \cite{MO2024,MW2014 .}
elements.sent-2411-079: The main results are stated as follows and they generalize well-known results on noncompact complete manifolds with nonnegative Ricci curvatures, see \cite{GW1981,Ya1976 for example.}
elements.sent-2411-080: There is no nontrivial $L^2$-integrable harmonic 1-form on noncompact complete gradient steady Ricci solitions.
elements.sent-2411-081: There is no nontrivial $L^2$-integrable harmonic 1-form on noncompact complete gradient shrinking K\"{a hler-Ricci solitions.}
elements.sent-2411-082: It is known that the Liouville type property for harmonic functions can be used to investigate the geometry of complete manifolds at infinity.
elements.sent-2411-083: In this paper, we observe that the Liouviile type property of harmonic 1-forms can be used to study the fundamental group representations of flat vector bundles.
elements.sent-2411-084: In fact, Theorems \ref{1formsteady , \ref{1formshrink are applied to distinguish certain flat vector bundles that arise from fundamental group representations into $\SL(r,\mathbb{C )$, see Section 3.}}}
elements.sent-2411-085: This is another motivation of our research.
elements.sent-2411-086: Let $(M,g,f)$ be a noncompact complete gradient Ricci soliton.
elements.sent-2411-087: In \cite{Ha1995 , Hamilton showed that the scalar curvature $\Sca_g$ satisfies \begin{equation}\begin{split}\label{GRS} \Sca_g+|\nabla f|^2-2\rho f=\lambda, \end{split}\end{equation} for a constant $\lambda$.}
elements.sent-2411-088: If $\rho=0$ (that is $\Ric_g=\nabla df$), since every steady soliton is in particular an ancient solution to the Ricci flow, we have (see \cite{Ca2010,Ch2009 ) \begin{equation}\begin{split}\label{steady1} \Sca_g\geq0,\ \lambda\geq0,\ |\nabla f|\leq\sqrt{\lambda}. \end{split}\end{equation} On the other hand, Munteanu-Se\v{s um \cite{MS2013 proved the volume growth \begin{equation}\begin{split}\label{steady2} \frac{r}{c_1}\leq\vol_g(B_p(r))\leq c_1e^{\sqrt{r}},\ r>r_0, \end{split}\end{equation} for two constants $c_1$, $r_0$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-089: Next let us consider the case $\rho=\frac{1 {2}$ (that is $\Ric_g+\nabla df=\frac{1 {2}\rho g$), after suitable change of $f$ we may assume \begin{equation}\begin{split}\label{shrinking1} \Sca_g+|\nabla f|^2=f. \end{split}\end{equation} It is known from \cite{Ca2010,Ch2009 that $\Sca_g\geq0$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-090: On the other hand, Cao-Zhou \cite{CZ2010 proved that for any fixed $p\in M$, we have \begin{equation}\begin{split}\label{shrinking2} \frac{1}{4}(\dist(\bullet,p)-c_2)^2\leq f\leq\frac{1}{4}(\dist(\bullet,p)+c_2)^2, \end{split}\end{equation} for a constant $c_2$.}

elements.sent-2411-116: Then we say that the flat vector bundle $E$ arises from the fundamental group representation $\rho$.
